claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and does not live with you.
  - Charlie is 10 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 18 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! To get started, I need to know a bit about your child (or children):

**How old is your child?**

This is important because:
- Children **under 16** generally qualify automatically
- Children **aged 16-19** may qualify if they're in approved education or training
- Children **aged 20 or over** typically don't qualify

Please tell me your child's age, and if you have multiple children, please tell me about each of them (their names and ages would be helpful).

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hi, thanks for helping me with this! I've got three kids I'd like to ask about.

There's Alex who's 3, Blake who's 8, and Charlie who's 10. I'm living with Alex and Charlie at home, but Blake doesn't live with me at the moment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'm happy to help you with Alex (3 years old), Blake (8 years old), and Charlie (10 years old).

Since all three children are under 16, they fall into the automatic qualification age category. However, I need to gather some more information about each child's situation.

Let me start with some important questions:

**1. First, about Blake (8 years old) who doesn't live with you:**
   - Are you still responsible for Blake in some way (for example, do you contribute to their upkeep or maintain parental responsibility)?
   - Where is Blake currently living? (For example, with another parent, in care, with relatives, etc.)

**2. For Alex (3) and Charlie (10) who live with you:**
   - Are either of them working full-time? (This is very unlikely at their ages, but I need to check as it's part of the eligibility criteria)

**3. General question for all three children:**
   - Are you the person responsible for each of them? (This means you're looking after them and contributing at least the same amount as Child Benefit would provide toward their upkeep)

Let's start with these questions, and I'll guide you through the rest of the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Right, so about Blake - he's living with his mum at the moment. I'm not really contributing anything financially right now, to be honest. It's been a difficult situation.

As for Alex and Charlie, no they're definitely not working! They're just little ones at home with me.

And yes, I'm responsible for Alex and Charlie - I look after them full-time and pay for everything they need. For Blake, like I said, his mum has him and I'm not able to contribute at the moment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for being so honest about your situation. Let me work through each child's eligibility based on what you've told me.

Let me check a few more important details:

**For Alex (3) and Charlie (10) - who live with you:**

1. **Are you currently living in the UK?** And have you been living in the UK for a certain period, or are you a UK citizen/have settled status?

2. **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex or Charlie?** (For example, another parent or guardian?)

**For Blake (8) - who lives with his mum:**

Since Blake is living with his mum and you're not contributing financially to his upkeep, it sounds like his mum would be the one who could claim Child Benefit for him, not you. 

In Child Benefit rules, generally only one person can claim for each child - typically the person the child lives with and who is responsible for them. Since you're not contributing to Blake's upkeep and he doesn't live with you, you wouldn't be eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake. His mother would be the appropriate person to claim for him.

Does that make sense? And could you answer those questions about Alex and Charlie so I can continue assessing your eligibility for them?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester and I'm a UK citizen, been here my whole life.

No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex or Charlie. They're both with me and I'm the one looking after them.

That makes sense about Blake - his mum would be the one to claim for him since he's living with her. I understand that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for clarifying! Let me continue with the assessment for Alex and Charlie.

Since you're a UK citizen living in Manchester, that satisfies the residency requirements. Now I need to check a couple more things:

**1. Immigration/Right to Recluse Check:**
Are Alex and Charlie:
- UK citizens, OR
- Do they have settled status/right to recluse in the UK?

(Given you're a UK citizen and they're your children living with you in the UK, they most likely qualify, but I need to confirm this)

**2. Presence in the UK:**
- Are Alex and Charlie currently in the UK with you?
- Have either of them been outside the UK for an extended period recently (like more than 8-12 weeks)?

**3. Education/Care situation:**
- Are Alex and Charlie in any kind of full-time, non-advanced education that's paid for by someone responsible for them? (This is a specific exclusion rule, though it's uncommon for children their age)

Once I have these answers, I'll be able to give you a final determination on your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex and Charlie!

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Yes, Alex and Charlie are both UK citizens - they were both born here.

They're both here in the UK with me, they haven't been abroad or anything like that.

As for education - well, Alex is only 3, so he just goes to nursery a couple of days a week. Charlie's 10, so he's at primary school. Just normal state school, nothing special or paid for privately or anything like that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent! That's very helpful. Let me just verify one final important detail:

**Hospital or Care Institution:**
Are Alex or Charlie currently in hospital, or in any residential care or institutional setting (like a care home or secure accommodation)?

(I'm assuming the answer is no since you mentioned they're at home with you, but I need to confirm this as part of the assessment)

Once you confirm this, I should have all the information I need to give you a final decision on your Child Benefit eligibility for Alex and Charlie!

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, they're not in hospital or anything like that. They're both at home with me, perfectly healthy thankfully.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Thank you for providing all that information. Let me now give you the final assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## **Your Child Benefit Eligibility Assessment - Final Results**

### ✅ **ELIGIBLE** for Child Benefit

**Great news!** You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for **Alex (3)** and **Charlie (10)**.

---

### **Individual Assessment:**

**✅ Alex (age 3) - ELIGIBLE**
- Under 16 (automatic qualification)
- Lives with you and you're fully responsible for them
- UK citizen living in the UK
- No one else is claiming for Alex
- Not in hospital or residential care
- Attending nursery (not an exclusion)

**✅ Charlie (age 10) - ELIGIBLE**
- Under 16 (automatic qualification)
- Lives with you and you're fully responsible for them
- UK citizen living in the UK
- No one else is claiming for Charlie
- Not in hospital or residential care
- Attending state primary school (not an exclusion)

**❌ Blake (age 8) - NOT ELIGIBLE (for you to claim)**
- Blake lives with his mother, not with you
- You are not currently contributing to Blake's upkeep
- Only one person can claim Child Benefit per child - the person who is responsible for them and who they live with
- **Blake's mother would be the appropriate person to claim for him**

---

### **Next Steps:**

1. **Apply for Child Benefit** for Alex and Charlie through GOV.UK or by post
2. You'll need to provide birth certificates for both children
3. You can backdate your claim for up to 3 months, so apply as soon as possible

**Important note about income:** If you (or your partner) earn over £50,000 a year, you may need to pay a tax charge (High Income Child Benefit Charge). You can still claim but might choose not to receive payments - however, it's still worth claiming as it protects National Insurance credits for your State Pension.

If your situation with Blake changes in the future (for example, if he comes to live with you and you become responsible for him), you could potentially claim for him at that point.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about applying for Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex (age 3) meets all eligibility criteria: (1) Under 16 years old - automatic age qualification, (2) Not working full-time, (3) You are responsible for Alex and contribute to their upkeep, (4) Alex lives with you, (5) UK citizen living in the UK - satisfies residency requirements, (6) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, (7) Not in hospital or residential care, (8) Not in full-time paid-for non-advanced education. Therefore, you are ELIGIBLE to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

Charlie (age 10) meets all eligibility criteria: (1) Under 16 years old - automatic age qualification, (2) Not working full-time, (3) You are responsible for Charlie and contribute to their upkeep, (4) Charlie lives with you, (5) UK citizen living in the UK - satisfies residency requirements, (6) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie, (7) Not in hospital or residential care, (8) Attending normal state primary school (not full-time paid-for non-advanced education). Therefore, you are ELIGIBLE to claim Child Benefit for Charlie.

Blake (age 8) does not meet the eligibility criteria for you to claim: While Blake is under 16 years old (automatic age qualification), (1) Blake does not live with you - he lives with his mother, (2) You are not contributing financially to Blake's upkeep, (3) You are not responsible for Blake in the way required for Child Benefit. Child Benefit can only be claimed by one person per child - typically the person the child lives with and who is responsible for them. In Blake's case, his mother would be the appropriate person to claim Child Benefit for him. Therefore, you are NOT ELIGIBLE to claim Child Benefit for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK citizen living in Manchester with three children: Alex (3), Charlie (10), and Blake (8). Alex and Charlie live with the claimant who is fully responsible for them and no one else is claiming for them. Both children meet all eligibility criteria. Blake lives with his mother and the claimant does not contribute to his upkeep, so Blake's mother would be the appropriate claimant for him. Overall result: ELIGIBLE for Alex and Charlie, NOT ELIGIBLE for Blake.
